rationale: Abstract X specifies the precise clinical gap (tachycardia terminating by pacing), presents all diagnostic statistics in a readable structure, and calibrates its conclusion appropriately to a prospective design, whereas Abstract Y is vaguer in its gap statement, has a convoluted results section, and ends with an under-confident conclusion that misrepresents the strength of the evidence.
loser_weakness: Abstract Y most lacked certainty calibration — its conclusion ('was useful and effective') fails to convey the high diagnostic accuracy demonstrated in a prospective study and reads as an under-claim relative to the 96-100% sensitivity/specificity results.
